title: Add --layers and --no-cache to buildah bud
body:
This patch adds in the caching feature to buildah bud.

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Working on tests, completions and docs.
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat  can you please pull these changes in and play around with it a bit. I have tried to hit every case I could find, but it is possible i missed/messed up something.
@umohnani8 looks like test unhappiness.  I'm pulling now to test fwiw.

Looks like `buildah from scratch` isn't happy with this patch.  The other tests that don't use scratch in the baseline tests passed with this patch though.

```
# buildah from scratch
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x38 pc=0xa136db]

goroutine 1 [running]:
```

Missing man pages and command completion.
Still missing man pages and completions.
Using filepath.Glob instead for pattern matching. Added docs, completions, and tests.
LGTM, going run it through the baseline tests.

:pushpin: Commit d3e3c69 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan d3e3c6976daf8196385f5ca1da59f1bad561dadf -->
I'd a test machine hiccup with Docker, but after I fixed that, the baseline tests passed.
:hourglass: Testing commit d3e3c69 with merge ad713d8...

Wouldn't caching be also useful for "normal" builds, when not using a Dockerfile? 
I am heavily using bash scripts for building my containers. 

/cc @jeremyeder

@zvonkok Open an issue for this and we can talk about it.  
You can do buildah commit, whenever you want and then base the next image off of the content.  So you have control over this in your scripts.
